name: Where do you write code?
description: Your primary coding environment — the IDE where you spend most of your time
suggest_reason: The default AI-first editor — AI pair programming built in from day one

name: Who runs your terminal tasks?
description: A CLI agent you direct to handle multi-step coding tasks autonomously
suggest_reason: Best-in-class terminal agent for autonomous, multi-file coding tasks

name: Who builds autonomously for you?
description: A fully autonomous agent you assign tasks to — it handles entire features or PRs

name: How does your AI reason and plan?
description: The framework that structures how your AI thinks, uses tools, and coordinates agents
suggest_reason: Production-grade state machine — the right foundation for complex agents

name: How do your AI calls chain together?
description: The pipeline layer that connects LLM calls, retrieval, and data processing into a workflow
suggest_reason: Lightweight and easy to start — integrates with any model provider

name: Which models does your stack route through?
description: A gateway that normalizes calls across providers — one API for all models, with fallbacks
suggest_reason: One interface for 100+ models — swap providers without rewriting your code

name: Where do your models actually run?
description: LLM providers and inference servers — where the actual model computation happens
suggest_reason: Claude powers most serious AI stacks — state of the art reasoning and coding

name: What knowledge does your AI have?
description: The memory layer — stores and retrieves vector embeddings for RAG and semantic search
suggest_reason: Easiest vector DB to get started — runs locally with no infrastructure

name: How do you see what's happening?
description: Traces every LLM call, eval, and cost so you know exactly what your stack is doing
suggest_reason: OSS, one line to add — you can't improve what you can't see

name: How does your UI get built?
description: Converts designs, prompts, or Figma files into production-ready UI components
suggest_reason: Prompt → production React/Tailwind in seconds — the fastest UI starting point

name: Who handles your code quality?
description: Automates code review, PR triage, test generation, and security scanning
suggest_reason: AI code review on every PR — catches bugs and style issues before they merge

name: What tools can your AI reach?
description: MCP servers that give your AI agent access to browsers, APIs, repos, and more
suggest_reason: Build an MCP server in minutes — the fastest way to give your agent new capabilities

name: How do you know it's working?
description: Tests, evals, and experiment tracking to measure and improve your AI output quality
suggest_reason: Catch output regressions before users do — CI-friendly and OSS

name: How does your team stay in sync?
description: AI-powered documentation that writes itself and stays current with your codebase

name: How do you manage what gets built?
description: AI tools for prioritization, roadmapping, and turning user feedback into decisions

name: How do you define what you're building?
description: Spec writing, API design, architecture diagrams, and contract-first tooling — the upstream layer before code is written
